definition: Artificial Intelligence is technology that enables computers to perform tasks that normally need human intelligence — understanding language, recognizing patterns, and making predictions or decisions from data.
React is a JavaScript library (built by Meta) for building user interfaces out of reusable components. It's one of the most widely used front-end tools for web and mobile apps.
Laravel is a PHP web framework used to build server-side web applications quickly, with built-in tools for routing, databases, authentication, and more.

SEO (Search Engine Optimization) is the practice of improving a website's or listing's visibility in search results — through keywords, content quality, technical structure, and backlinks — so it ranks higher and gets more organic traffic.

UI (User Interface) is how a product looks and is laid out; UX (User Experience) is how it feels to use — how intuitive, efficient, and enjoyable the journey is. Good UI/UX design increases engagement and conversions.
An API (Application Programming Interface) is a defined way for two pieces of software to talk to each other — for example, letting a website pull live data from another service.

Cloud Computing means running software, storage, and computing power over the internet (on servers like AWS, Google Cloud, or Azure) instead of on a local machine — so it scales on demand and is accessible from anywhere.
E-Commerce is the buying and selling of goods or services online — through marketplaces like Amazon, or a brand's own store like Shopify.
Website Development is the process of building a website — covering front-end (what visitors see and interact with) and back-end (servers, databases, business logic) work.
Branding is shaping how a business is perceived — its name, logo, visual identity, tone of voice, and the promise it makes to customers — so it's recognizable and trusted.
Digital Marketing is promoting a product or business through online channels — SEO, paid ads (PPC), social media, email, and content — to reach and convert customers.
PPC (Pay-Per-Click) is a form of online advertising where you pay only when someone clicks your ad — common on Google, Amazon, and social platforms.
FBA (Fulfillment by Amazon) is Amazon's program where sellers ship inventory to Amazon's warehouses, and Amazon handles storage, packing, shipping, and customer service for those orders.
